target.title: [Feature]: feat(secrets): non-interactive `secrets migrate --auto` for automation scripts
target.body:
### Summary

Need non-interactive `openclaw secrets migrate --auto` to automate secret rotation in upgrade scripts, replacing the manual `secrets configure --apply` wizard.


### Problem to solve

## Problem

Related: #33702

`openclaw secrets configure --apply` requires interactive TTY with manual field-by-field selection. This breaks automated workflows like upgrade scripts.

Current chain: **scan (auto) → select (manual) → apply (auto)** — the middle step kills automation.

## Proposed Solution

```bash
openclaw secrets migrate --strategy env --auto --dry-run
openclaw secrets migrate --strategy env --auto --yes
openclaw secrets migrate --strategy env --category models,plugins --auto --yes

Behavior:

Scan all supported credential fields (reuse audit logic)
Auto-generate SecretRefs from selected strategy (env → derive var name from field path)
For env: output setx commands or append to .env
Validate all refs resolve before commit
Atomic write with rollback on failure
Idempotent — safe to re-run
Use Case
Windows automated upgrade script:
npm install -g openclaw@latest
openclaw plugins update --all
openclaw gateway install --force
openclaw gateway stop
openclaw gateway restart
openclaw secrets migrate --strategy env --auto --yes
openclaw doctor --lint --json

Without this, the upgrade script leaves plaintext secrets in config and users must manually run secrets configure afterward — which they often forget.

Environment
OpenClaw on Windows (npm global)
Gateway port 18789
Providers: DeepSeek, Google, Groq
Channels: Telegram, Discord




### Proposed solution

Add `openclaw secrets migrate --strategy env --auto --yes` that auto-scans all plaintext credentials, generates SecretRefs, validates resolution, and applies atomically — no TTY required. Extend #33702's `secrets set` concept to full batch migration.


### Alternatives considered

_No response_

### Impact

Affects all users running automated OpenClaw deployments. Currently plaintext secrets remain in config indefinitely because migration requires manual TTY interaction. Blocks CI/CD pipelines, multi-instance management, and upgrade script automation.


### Evidence/examples

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_
target.author: braincat
